intro.uz: David Deidaning “The Way of the Superior Man” kitobi asosida: buyuk inson boʻlish — qoʻrquvni tan olib, uning ustidan oʻtishdir. Har bir kuchli odam qoʻrqadi, lekin oʻz chegarasidan (edge) oʻtib, orzusi tomon harakat qiladi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi: maqsad infinitivi (to / in order to / so that — har ishni SABAB bilan qilish) va kelasi zamon shakllari (will / going to / hozirgi davomiy — rejalarni aytish). Kristin haqidagi POV hikoyada kelajak shakllarini bevosita eshitasiz.
intro.en: Based on David Deida's “The Way of the Superior Man”: becoming a great person means admitting your fear and going past it. Every strong person is afraid, but they step past their edge toward their dream. This week's two grammar topics: the infinitive of purpose (to / in order to / so that — doing everything FOR a reason) and the future forms (will / going to / present continuous — stating your plans). You'll hear the future forms directly in the POV story about Kristin.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida har bir harakatingizning sababini “to / in order to / so that” bilan toʻxtamasdan ayta olasiz va kelajak rejalaringizni toʻgʻri shaklda (I'm going to… / I'll… / I'm …ing) tasvirlab bera olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can state the reason for each action with “to / in order to / so that” without pausing, and describe your future plans with the right form (I'm going to… / I'll… / I'm …ing).

grammar.titleUz: Maqsad infinitivi: to / in order to / so that
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Speaking & Writing — explain WHY you do something (purpose linkers)
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can express purpose with to / in order to / so that
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekcha “-ish uchun” / “maqsadida” doim “uchun” soʻzini oʻz ichiga oladi, shuning uchun oʻquvchilar inglizchada ham “for” ishlatib yuboradi. Ammo maqsad feʼl bilan aytilsa, ingliz tili “for + feʼl” EMAS, “to + feʼl” ishlatadi: “pul topish uchun” = “to earn money” (❗ “for earn” emas). “for” faqat ot bilan keladi: “for money”.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I bought a rocket for go to the moon. I study for improve. ✅ I bought a rocket to go to the moon. I study to improve. Maqsad feʼl bilan → “to + feʼl” (yoki “in order to”); “for” esa faqat ot bilan (for money).

examples.en: She bought a rocket to go to the moon.
examples.uz: U oyga borish uchun raketa sotib oldi.

examples.en: He left his safe job in order to live his dream.
examples.uz: U orzusi bilan yashash uchun tinch ishini tark etdi.

examples.en: Speak slowly so that everyone can understand you.

exercises.hintUz: maqsad feʼl bilan → to + feʼl; “for” faqat ot bilan

exercises.prompt: He faces his fear ___ live his dream.
exercises.hintUz: rasmiyroq maqsad shakli: in order to + feʼl
egasi boshqa / modal (can) kerak → so that + ega + feʼl

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): bugun qilgan uch ishingizni sababi bilan — “I … to … . I … in order to … . I … so that … .”
exercises.hintUz: maqsad feʼl bilan → to / in order to; egasi boshqa boʻlsa → so that

grammar.titleUz: Kelasi zamon shakllari: will / going to / hozirgi davomiy
grammar.titleEn: Future forms — will / going to / present continuous
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 & 2 — talk about your plans and predictions naturally
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can choose will / going to / present continuous for the right meaning
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida kelajak asosan bitta shakl bilan beriladi (qilaman, boraman), shuning uchun oʻquvchilar hamma narsaga “will” ishlatadi. Ingliz tili esa maʼnoga qarab tanlaydi: oldindan qaror qilingan reja → going to; vaqti belgilangan uchrashuv → hozirgi davomiy (I'm meeting…); shu ondagi qaror, vaʼda yoki bashorat → will.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I will visit my family this weekend, I already bought the tickets. ✅ I'm going to visit / I'm visiting my family this weekend. Reja oldindan tayyor (chipta olingan) → going to / present continuous; “will” esa shu ondagi qaror va bashorat uchun.

examples.en: I'm going to start my own business.

examples.uz: Xavotir olmang — yordam beraman (shu ondagi qaror).

examples.en: I'm meeting my mentor tomorrow at five.
examples.uz: Ertaga soat beshda ustozim bilan uchrashaman (belgilangan uchrashuv).

exercises.prompt: I've decided — I ___ start my own business next year.
exercises.hintUz: oldindan qaror qilingan reja → am going to

exercises.prompt: The phone is ringing. Don't move — I ___ answer it.
I ___ my mentor tomorrow at five.

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting: kelasi yilingiz haqida bir reja va bir vaʼda — “Next year I'm going to … . Whatever happens, I'll … .”
exercises.hintUz: reja → going to / present continuous; vaʼda yoki bashorat → will

vocab.uz: yaqin, samimiy munosabat (eng yaqin insonlar bilan)
vocab.example: Pick an area of your life — perhaps your intimate relationship.

vocab.uz: maʼnaviy, ruhiy (shaxsiy eʼtiqod, tashkiliy dinga bogʻliq emas)
vocab.defEn: about personal belief and inner experience, not organized religion
vocab.example: She wanted to go to the moon for spiritual reasons.

vocab.uz: kun koʻrmoq, tirikchilik uchun pul topmoq
vocab.defEn: to make money, usually through work
vocab.example: How do you earn a living?

vocab.uz: chegara, oxirgi nuqta (bu yerda: qoʻrquv chegarasi)
vocab.defEn: a limit or boundary; the point where you stop
vocab.example: Your edge is where you stop because of fear.

vocab.defEn: to weaken or damage something valuable
to give energy to something and let it grow, like spoiling a child
vocab.example: Do you cater to your fears or push past them?

vocab.defEn: to lie to or fool yourself
vocab.example: If you say you're not afraid, you're deluding yourself.

vocab.defEn: to go with someone or happen together with something
vocab.example: Bob the penguin accompanied Kristin to the moon.

vocab.defEn: to get near your limit, where fear begins
vocab.example: Fear comes when you approach your edge.

vocab.defEn: to hesitate or wait instead of taking action
vocab.example: Are you hanging back instead of chasing your dream?

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Syuzan keyingi semestr uchun fanlarga roʻyxatdan oʻtadi va psixologiyadan qoʻshimcha yoʻnalish (minor) olmoqchi boʻladi. Bu — Superior Man gʻoyasiga mos: oʻsish uchun oʻzingizni qiynash, qoʻshimcha yuk olib chegarangizdan oʻtish. Sizningcha, u shuncha fanni bir semestrda bitira oladimi, yoki oʻz chegarasidan oshib ketadimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Susan registers for next semester's courses and wants a minor in psychology. It fits the Superior Man theme: pushing yourself and stepping past your edge in order to grow. Do you think she can handle all those subjects in one semester, or is she going past her limit?

keyVocab.defEn: to officially put your name on a list for something
the main field a student studies at university
a second, smaller field of study alongside your major
points you count towards your degree at university
a timetable; a list of appointments and times
the fees you pay for education

quiz.q: Which profession does the surname Bond originally come from?
quiz.qUz: “Bond” familiyasi asli qaysi kasbdan kelib chiqqan?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: a) Farmers (dehqonlar). Neil c) Guards deb xato qildi. Aslida “Bond”lar bir zodagonga biriktirilgan dehqonlar (peasant farmers) boʻlgan — josuslik oʻyini ancha keyin paydo boʻlgan.

vocab.uz: portfel-karyera (bir vaqtda bir nechta ish bilan shugʻullanish)
vocab.defEn: a working life made up of several different part-time jobs or roles

vocab.uz: zoʻrgʻa kun koʻrmoq, daromadni xarajatga yetkazmoq
vocab.defEn: to earn just enough money to live on
someone who starts and runs businesses
doing the same thing again and again (a serial entrepreneur)
relating to a big company; a corporate job is a job at a large corporation

vocab.en: to add another string to your bow
vocab.defEn: to learn a new skill you can use in the future

funEnglish.title: How to Face Your Fears and Live Your Dream

speakingPrompt.en: Think of one big dream you have. What are you going to do to reach it, and what fear will you have to push past? Explain the reason behind each step.
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: maqsad infinitivi (I study to…, I work hard in order to…, … so that …) va kelasi zamon shakllari (I'm going to…, I'll…, I'm …ing).
